mission.title: Install and Configure RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
mission.description: The RabbitMQ Cluster Operator is a Kubernetes operator designed to deploy and manage RabbitMQ clusters efficiently. It automates the lifecycle management of RabbitMQ, including creation, upgrades, and graceful shutdowns. This operator simplifies RabbitMQ deployment in Kubernetes environments but requires familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add rabbitmq https://charts.rabbitmq.com

helm repo update

```

> This is the official repository for RabbitMQ charts.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator using Helm, which manages the deployment of RabbitMQ clusters.

```bash

helm install rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --version 2.20.1 --namespace rabbitmq --create-namespace

```

> Installs the operator in the 'rabbitmq' namespace.

steps.title: Deploy a RabbitMQ cluster
steps.description:
This step deploys a sample RabbitMQ cluster using a predefined configuration file.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rabbitmq/cluster-operator/main/docs/examples/hello-world/rabbitmq.yaml

```

> Deploys a sample RabbitMQ cluster.

steps.title: Verify the RabbitMQ cluster deployment
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the RabbitMQ cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq

kubectl get rabbitmqclusters -n rabbitmq

```

> Lists all pods in the 'rabbitmq' namespace to verify their status.
> Lists RabbitMQ clusters to check their status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if the RabbitMQ clusters are not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall rabbitmq-operator --namespace rabbitmq

kubectl delete namespace rabbitmq

```

> Uninstalls the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your RabbitMQ data. This step involves upgrading the operator and verifying the deployment.

```bash

kubectl get rabbitmqclusters -n rabbitmq -o yaml > backup-rabbitmqclusters.yaml

helm upgrade rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --version 2.20.1 --namespace rabbitmq

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq

```

> Backs up the current RabbitMQ cluster configuration.
> Upgrades the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator.
> Verifies that the RabbitMQ pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get rabbitmqclusters -n rabbitmq -o yaml > backup-rabbitmqclusters.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create RabbitMQ cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The RabbitMQ Cluster Operator may not have sufficient permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure the operator has the necessary RBAC permissions configured.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'RabbitMQ cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The RabbitMQ pods may be in a crash loop due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n rabbitmq' for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl apply failed: resource already exists'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The RabbitMQ cluster resource may already exist in the namespace.

**Fix:**
Use 'kubectl delete rabbitmqcluster <name> -n rabbitmq' to remove the existing resource before reapplying.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to RabbitMQ'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Network policies may be blocking access to the RabbitMQ service.

**Fix:**
Check and update network policies to allow traffic to the RabbitMQ service.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for RabbitMQ 4.1.3, improved handling of RabbitMQ cluster upgrades.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the RabbitMQ pods in the 'Running' state when executing 'kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and the necessary tools installed.
